_comment_2: Tyk Classic API definition for p08-req-headers. The canonical contract is: gateway adds X-Bench-In: 1 to upstream request and drops X-Forwarded-For from upstream request. Other client-supplied headers must pass through unchanged.
_comment_3: transform_headers in extended_paths runs in Tyk's middleware chain BEFORE the reverse-proxy hop. Adding X-Bench-In works (the upstream sees it). Deleting X-Forwarded-For at this layer is, however, immediately undone by Tyk's reverse proxy (gateway/reverse_proxy.go ~ WrappedServeHTTP), which unconditionally re-stamps X-Forwarded-For with the request's RemoteAddr right before the upstream call — there is no native config knob in Classic OSS to opt out.
_comment_4: We declare the delete anyway so the matrix shows the intent; the architectural deviation is documented in NOTES.md and lands as a probe FAIL on the canonical fixture.
_comment_5: Wildcard path `.*` makes transform_headers apply to every request the API serves — necessary because the fixture probes /headers (not /anything).
name: bench
